codeReview.summary: The fix stays within the intended root reload seam: it removes the last storage-table fallback when normalizing root id strings, adds a focused negative-boundary proof for plain root strings, and leaves the required RecordId-backed reload/delete and create_at/upsert_at table-identity behaviors to the existing targeted regressions referenced in the handoff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker skill still describes a strict fail-first test workflow, but this fix feature reused the focused regressions already named in the feature verification steps and only added the missing negative-boundary proof. That procedure fit the mission, yet the transcript still records skill non-compliance.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl session a4f984c4-0918-4605-9859-1e32cb19b9b7 shows skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false solely because GitNexus tooling was unavailable; the rest of the transcript shows the worker using the exact focused regressions named by the feature plus the new plain_root_strings_are_not_coerced_into_record_ids_on_reload proof. The current rust-library-worker SKILL.md still presents fail-first test creation as a blanket step even for narrowing/revalidation-style follow-up fixes.

summary: Pass. Commit d15bb0bc71e2c7bf4b8dd30f2b85e0ba605d86ee cleanly removes the root storage-table fallback from the shared reload/decode boundary, adds the missing negative-boundary regression for unrelated plain root strings, and stays aligned with the feature scope without introducing new regressions in the preserved RecordId paths.
